item.title: As prime minister, Mark Carney makes Question Period a low priority
item.description: A Global News analysis of Hansard shows Prime Minister Mark Carney has attended Question Period less than 30 per cent of the time, much less than either of his two predecessors.

item.title: Ottawa's big bet on world's largest cricket farm ran into a simple problem: the 'yuck factor'
item.description: Ottawa backed the world's largest cricket farm with tens of millions in public money. It collapsed before reaching full production, exposing a gap between hype and demand while also leaving questions about how much of that money was ever recovered.

item.title: NDP leadership vote live: Candidates square off to become the New Democrats’ new leader
item.description: Follow the Star for live updates as the embattled NDP selects its next leader at the Winnipeg Convention.

item.title: Prime Minister Carney plans to attend Sunday’s Juno Awards
Energy policy key as NDP leadership candidates make final pitch
Avi Lewis previews his leadership vision as New Democrats express mix of unease, excitement
Toronto doctor stripped of licence for writing improper COVID-19 exemptions
Man charged with second-degree murder in East York stabbing death of mechanic
China denies forced labour allegations amid fallout from Michael Ma’s comments
Ontario Premier Doug Ford heading to Texas to talk trade and tariffs
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford will push a pro-trade and anti-tariff message during a multi-day trip to Texas, U.S. where he will meet Governor Greg Abbott, an ally of President Donald Trump, and business leaders.

item.title: Winning matters, Manitoba premier warns federal New Democrats
Nate Erskine-Smith urges Liberal rivals to step aside in Scarborough Southwest byelection
item.description: The MP for Beaches—East York knows losing the byelection nomination could derail his campaign to be the next leader of the Ontario Liberals.

item.title: Feminists urge continuing fight of slain Iraqi-Canadian, Yanar Mohammed
Ontario’s budget banks on a housing resale rebound in 2026 — but real estate players aren’t so sure
Man rushed to hospital after collision involving motorcycle on Hwy. 427 in Toronto
2026 FIFA World Cup will make Toronto even busier. Here’s how the city plans to keep people moving
item.description: To handle the extra people expected during the games, the city plans on traffic restrictions, increased transit service — and more bicycles for rent.

item.title: NDP leadership candidates to make their final pitch to voters at Winnipeg convention
item.description: The NDP leadership candidates are making their last pitch to party members before voting in the six-month campaign concludes later today.

item.title: Mark Carney’s strategy for the public service becomes clearer
item.description: It’s not a public-service overhaul, it’s a workaround

item.title: Bill C-12 curbing asylum, immigration rights becomes law amid warnings of court challenges
item.description: Law fast-tracks deportation for some asylum seekers and allows Ottawa to share immigration information with provinces

item.title: With finish line in sight, NDP leadership candidates try to win over voters
item.description: The five candidates vying for the NDP's leadership into the next election gave one final appeal to members before the party chooses its next leader.

item.title: Federal New Democrats are in Manitoba — but do Prairie NDP leaders want them there?
item.description: Despite Premier Wab Kinew delivering words of affirmation to New Democrats gathered in Winnipeg, sources says the Manitoba NDP has tried to distance itself from the federal wing of the party, as tensions with Prairie New Democrats are once again playing out in the leadership race.

item.title: Should gambling taxes fund sports? Former Olympian says it's a 'devil's bargain'
item.description: The government will be making a "devil's bargain" if it moves ahead with a recommendation to boost revenue for sport organizations through taxes on sports gambling, former track and field Olympian Bruce Kidd said.

item.title: Canadian Army overhaul looms as DND finalizes mobilization plans
item.description: Canada's army is preparing a sweeping reorganization just as the National Defence Department moves to finalize its mobilization plan. Senior commanders warn the current force is ill suited to a more dangerous world, with proposals under study to expand reserves and generate hundreds of thousands of additional personnel in a crisis.

item.title: What happens after communism? Cubans face huge challenges, big decisions
item.description: Cuba appears to be tottering toward some kind of political transition. Some Cubans are concerned that the residents own nothing and are too impoverished to take over the few functioning businesses on the island, while there is a large and wealthy diaspora community (and potentially also foreign investors) who would be able to swoop in and buy property and businesses at bargain prices.

item.title: Conservatives put pressure on Carney to clarify his position on forced labour in China
item.description: Move follows comments by Liberal MP Michael Ma that appeared to cast doubt that the practice takes place

item.title: Métis National Council president says RCMP statement of regret falls short
item.description: The president of the Métis National Council says the RCMP's response to an extensive spying program that targeted hundreds of Indigenous people falls short.

item.title: Provincial supervisor announces first round of cuts at Toronto Catholic school board. Here’s what is targeted
Politics Insider: Ottawa opens public sector buyout applications
item.description: And, the federal NDP leadership race heads to the finish line

item.title: Canada has 200 unfilled positions for air traffic controllers
item.description: A deadly Air Canada crash Sunday in New York has drawn attention to U.S. air traffic controller shortages, where more than 3,000 jobs are vacant.

item.title: Opposition fears Doug Ford's omnibus budget bill will be fast-tracked through to law
Canada will cancel thousands of refugee claims under new retroactive law
item.description: Immigration and borders legislation known as Bill C-12 has received royal assent. It will mean thousands of asylum seekers have their claims t…

item.title: Carney government sidesteps questions on forced labour in China
Sunshine List of $100,000 earners in public sector tops 400,000 for first time
Air Canada CEO's lack of respect outweighs his lack of French, crisis management expert says
item.description: The CEO of Canada’s largest airline remains under fire from multiple fronts this week following a botched expression of condolence and a subsequent fumbled apology.

item.title: 'This is a dumb war,' Manitoba Premier Wab Kinew says in letter urging PM to take action to stop Iran conflict
item.description: Manitoba's premier is calling on Mark Carney to help stop the war in Iran.

item.title: Toronto Humber Yacht Club shut down by council — even as club members vow ‘it’s not over’
Today at Toronto city council: City to pay residents to shovel snow, orders Humber Yacht Club to close
item.description: The Star's city hall team will be updating throughout the council session that is scheduled to run Wednesday through Friday.

item.title: NDP says goodbye to the past, ponders what the future holds
item.description: New Democrats gave tribute to NDP former leader Jagmeet Singh on Friday — just days before they're set to announce his successor.

item.title: New Democrats gather in Winnipeg to pick new leader, plot path out of political exile
Israel pushes back against Canada’s criticism of its occupation of southern Lebanon
item.description: The IDF has to intervene because the Lebanese government can’t resolve the threat posed by Hezbollah, Israel’s deputy foreign minister says

item.title: George Brown college files ‘mass termination’ notice in face of steep drop in enrolment
item.description: The Toronto college has indicated it is laying off 22 hourly and 29 salaried employees amid budget pressures.

item.title: A sign Toronto’s election is unofficially underway? Attack ads target ‘failed mayor’ Olivia Chow
item.description: The ads from A Better City Toronto hammer Mayor Olivia Chow over her record on public safety, affordability and gridlock.

item.title: Outlook for Ontario housing starts weakens further away from 1.5 million goal
item.description: Ontario’s housing start projections have been revised downward once again in the province’s budget, putting the government even farther off track from building 1.5 million homes over 10 years — a target to which the ministers in charge say they pay no mind.

item.title: Pierre Poilievre backs J.K. Rowling’s support for new Olympic gender policy
item.description: Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre is supporting a ruling this week by the International Olympic Committee that bans transgender women from women's sports at the Games.

item.title: Liberal MP's Chinese forced labour comments come as U.S. probes Canada's imports
item.description: Liberal MP Michael Ma's apology for confronting an academic witness about whether her evidence on Chinese aluminum supply chains could be "hearsay" coincides with a highly sensitive moment in Canada's trade war with the Trump administration. The U.S. included Canada in its recent tariff investigation to crack down on countries suspected of turning a blind eye to forced labour.

item.title: International visitors helped boost Canada’s tourism industry in 2025: data
item.description: The agency said Friday that spending by international tourists grew 3.6 per cent in the fourth quarter of 2025 from the previous quarter, which was the fastest pace in two years.

item.title: Ottawa opens applications for public-sector buyouts, sets July 24 deadline
item.description: Letters went out in December to around 68,000 workers informing them that they may be eligible for the early retirement incentive

item.title: Canada launches inquiry on frozen and canned vegetable imports
‘We’re fixing it’: Toronto unveils new congestion plan as gridlock continues to mount
item.description: Video

item.title: Doug Ford is taking his anti-tariff campaign to Texas
Hodgson defers questions on Michael Ma to Prime Minister Mark Carney
Feds launch early retirement program for public servants after months of delay
Platform doors on the TTC would save lives and money. Why does it not have them?
Alberta referendum ‘can have an effect’ on investor confidence, Carney says
item.description: When asked whether he thought an Alberta referendum could impact investor confidence, Carney said, 'It can, yeah. It can have an effect.'

item.title: Doug Ford’s Tories table record $244B Ontario budget amid hefty deficit
10 things to know about Ontario’s budget
Toronto city council votes to ban flying flags of foreign countries at City Hall, civic centres
item.description: Flags of foreign nations will not be raised on flagpoles at Toronto City Hall and civic centres starting in December.

item.title: Canada loaning millions to proposed Nunavik rare earth mining project linked to Trump White House
item.description: Amid Prime Minister Mark Carney’s calls that the Canada-U.S. relationship is ruptured, Ottawa has committed $175-million to a mining project in northern Quebec whose major U.S. investor is closely linked to the Trump administration.

item.title: What's new in Ottawa-Alberta deal to cut methane emissions from oil and gas sector
item.description: As part of the wide-ranging Canada-Alberta memorandum of understanding on energy policy and a new oil pipeline, the two sides announced an agreement-in-principle on new methane rules and targets. It could speed up methane reductions, experts say, but some questions remain.

item.title: At historic low point, New Democrats descend on Winnipeg to choose a new leader
item.description: The NDP will have a new leader by Sunday afternoon — and whoever comes out of the party's convention in Winnipeg with the top job will be tasked with bringing the party back from a historic low.

item.title: Liberal MP sorry after confusion over remarks on forced labour in China
item.description: Ma said he regretted making a mistake with his remarks, while pointing out that he had referred to the southern Chinese city of Shenzhen, and not Xinjiang.

item.title: Toronto city council gives green light to develop paid sidewalk shovelling program
item.description: Toronto residents could get paid to shovel their sidewalk as early as next winter.

item.title: Ontario to boost home care funding, may miss long-term care bed goal
item.description: Ontario is investing $1.1 billion more in home health care as it grapples with the reality that it is unlikely to achieve its goal of building 58,000 new long-term care beds by 2028.

item.title: Ontario deficit nearly doubles as province tables $244.2 billion budget in face of ‘unpredictable economic shocks’
item.description: Ontario’s projected deficit for the coming year has nearly doubled, with the Ford government now projecting that it will be $13.8 billion in the red in 2026-2027 amid “ongoing economic and geopolitical uncertainty.”

item.title: Liberal MP Michael Ma sparks backlash after casting doubt on forced labour of Uyghurs in China
item.description: Ma, who accompanied Carney on his Beijing trip, questioned an expert during a Commons committee meeting on whether her testimony was ‘hearsay’

item.title: Ottawa’s defence spending plan has ambition, but half of the equation is missing
item.description: Mark Carney has told us that all this spending is necessary, but what will Canada stop doing to make way for it?

item.title: Politics Insider: Canada hits NATO military spending target set in 2014
item.description: And, Ontario is projecting a larger-than-expected $13.8-billion deficit for the coming fiscal year

item.title: Floor-crossing Liberal MP apologizes for behaviour in committee meeting about Chinese EVs
item.description: Liberal MP Michael Ma is facing criticism for an aggressive set of questions at a parliamentary committee where he appeared to downplay forced labour in China.

item.title: Readers share their thoughts about Canada reaching NATO’s military spending targets
item.description: Whether they were glad Canada hit its target or concerned about additional spending, Globe readers had many thoughts on the subject

item.title: Cops at every station. Millions on anti-suicide doors. Inside one councillor’s TTC subway safety pitch — and why critics call it a stunt
Toronto ends practice of raising foreign flags at government buildings
Senate approves budget bill, allowing public-sector buyouts to proceed
item.description: Government’s 600-page bill includes provision for public-servant buyout incentives worth a combined $1.5-billion

item.title: Watchdog report prompts renewed calls for Indigenous people to run Indigenous procurement
item.description: Federal procurement watchdog found 'cascading failures' in the federal government’s administration of Indigenous procurement programs.

item.title: Ottawa proposes amendments to Elections Act to counter long-ballot protests
item.description: Bill C-25 targets such activism by limiting electors to signing one nomination form per candidate

item.title: Ontario 2026 budget highlights: small business tax cut, AI funding, LCBO revenue declining
item.description: The Ontario government tabled a budget Thursday that focused on developing a resilient economy amid geopolitical uncertainty. Here are the highlights.

item.title: The parliamentary budget officer is caught again in partisan conflict. Should MPs pick their own watchdog?
item.description: It's very possible the Conservatives will forget about their concerns as soon as the new PBO releases a report that is unflattering to the government. But ideally the appointment of an independent officer of Parliament would not be the subject of political conflict and partisan email campaigns.

item.title: Six takeaways from Ontario’s budget, from health care to HST
item.description: Budget predicts higher deficit for the coming fiscal year, gives small businesses a tax break and allocates more funding to hospitals

item.title: Alberta moves to implement interprovincial pact to ease trade rules on consumer goods
item.description: The agreement doesn't apply to alcohol, cannabis, food, live animals, tobacco or plants, and it lets provinces maintain certain restrictions on items for health and safety reasons.

item.title: Liberal government targets deepfakes, foreign interference in major election law reforms
item.description: The Liberal government is introducing reforms that it says will allow the country to better detect and protect elections from foreign and domestic interference, bribery, disinformation and a host of other potential threats to democracy.

item.title: Ontario budget adds $1.1B for home care
item.description: Ontario is lagging on its promise to build or upgrade 58,000 new long-term care beds by 2028, although due to the growing demand among older a…

item.title: New federal election bill seeks to crack down on deepfakes, long ballots
item.description: 'Canada's elections belong to Canadians. Outside money, influence and threats have no place in them,' MacKinnon said at a news conference on Parliament Hill Thursday afternoon.

item.title: Hospitals get $1.1B infusion from Ford government as critics warn it’s not enough
Toronto’s flawed awarding of snow contracts cost millions more than expected: audit
Canada could provide vessels, cyber support to clear Strait of Hormuz if there's a ceasefire: minister
item.description: Defence Minister David McGuinty says the federal government is considering sending vessels and cyber expertise to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz — the chokepoint at the centre of a burgeoning global energy crisis — if there is a ceasefire.

item.title: Ottawa proposes election bill that takes aim at deepfakes, long ballots
item.description: Government says Strong and Free Elections Act responds to recommendations from an inquiry into foreign interference and from others

item.title: Ottawa plans $1.7-billion boost in housing transfers to provinces, territories
item.description: Funds can be used to reduce development fees or levies on new home construction, government says

item.title: Toronto school boards want housing development on some land: Star readers discuss
Watchdog finds little oversight over multi-billion-dollar Indigenous procurement program
item.description: Procurement watchdog’s probe says Canada can’t 'credibly demonstrate' that Indigenous businesses are benefitting from multi-billion dollar procurement program.

item.title: Ottawa failed to enforce its own Indigenous contracting rules, procurement watchdog finds
item.description: Official criticizes ‘the disingenuousness’ of Ottawa’s goal of awarding 5% of federal contracts to Indigenous businesses

item.title: Canada’s military spending officially hits 2% of GDP, NATO says
item.description: Canada’s defence spending as a proportion of its economic output has not been this high since the end of the Cold War
